meta.focus: Review of Kanji, Vocabulary, and Grammar from Lessons 13–14.

sections.title: Part 1: Conversation Check
sections.instructions: Read each conversation and select the best answer.

items.title: Conversation 1
items.context: Yamamoto-sensei talks with Lee, an international student, about reading and writing Japanese at school.

lines.jp: リーさんは 毎日 日本語を 読んでいますか。
はい、毎日 少し 読んでいます。ゆっくり 読むのが すきです。
いいですね。毎日 手で 書いてください。よく 分かりますよ。
わかりました。手で 書くことも、電話で 話すことも すきです。

items.question: What does Yamamoto-sensei ask Lee to do every day?
items.explanation: Yamamoto-sensei says 毎日手で書いてください — 'Please write by hand every day.' 書いてください = te-form of 書く + ください = polite request (G7). 手 (N5.13) = hand; 書 (N5.13) = write; 手で = 'by hand' — v_te + p_de, where で marks the instrument/means. Lee replies わかりました = 'Understood' — polite past of 分かる (分 N5.4, read わか). Lee's Line 4 confirms liking both 手で書くこと (writing by hand) and 電話で話すこと (talking on the phone) — こと nominalizes each verb phrase (early-use from N5.9, written in hiragana as こと since 事 is N4.14).

items.title: Conversation 2
items.context: Nana and Rikizo talk in the school corridor about how much time they have today.

lines.jp: りきぞさん、今日は 時間が 多いですか。
いいえ、今日は 時間が 少ないです。電話で 友だちと 話しますから。
そうですか。たいてい、わたしは 時間が たくさん あります。
いいですね。わたしも たいてい 時間が 多いですが、今日は 少ないです。

items.question: Why does Rikizo say he has little time today?
choices: He is going to talk on the phone with a friend
He is going to write by hand
He has a test at school
He wants to read many books
items.answer: He is going to talk on the phone with a friend
items.explanation: りきぞ says 電話で友だちと話しますから — 'Because I am going to talk with my friend on the phone.' から (p_kara_because, G9) follows the polite verb form 話します to give a reason: verb/adjective + から = 'because.' 少ない (N5.14) = scarce/few; 多い (N5.14) = many — antonym i-adjectives. 時間 (N5.14): 時 (N5.4) + 間 (N5.14) = time. In Line 4, りきぞ uses p_ga_but (が after 多いです) to contrast: 'I usually have a lot [of time], but today I have little.' Note: this が is the clause connector 'but' (p_ga_but, G9) — different from the subject-marking が (p_ga) before 多いです in the same sentence.

items.title: Conversation 3 (Casual)
items.context: Rikizo and Yamakawa, close friends, chat casually in the school library after class.

lines.jp: ね、この 白い 本、読んだ？
うん、読んだよ。多分 この 本が いちばん おもしろいね。
うん。また 読みたいよ。いちばん おもしろかった。
ゆっくり 読むのが すきだよ。電話で 話すことも すきだけど。

items.question: What does Yamakawa say about the book?
choices: He read it and thinks it is the most interesting
He has not read it yet
He wants to write about the book
He read it but it was boring
items.answer: He read it and thinks it is the most interesting
items.explanation: やまかわ says うん、読んだよ。多分この本がいちばんおもしろいね — 'Yeah, I read it. This book is probably the most interesting.' 読んだ = plain past of 読む (G9) — casual confirmation. 多分 (N5.14) = probably. いちばん (N5.1) = most/best. おもしろい in plain form without です = casual predicate (i-adj drops です in casual speech, G9). In Line 4, ゆっくり読むのが好きだよ = 'I like reading slowly' — ゆっくり (N5.13) + 読む (dict. form for nominalization) + のが + 好きだ. 好きだ = 好き (na-adj) + だ (g_da, casual copula, G1/G9). けど (p_kedo, G9) as a sentence-final trailing-off = 'though / but' — a natural casual softening. In りきぞ's Line 3: 読みたい (plain_desire_tai, G9) = 'want to read' (casual desire); おもしろかった (plain_past_adj, G9) = 'was interesting' in plain past.

sections.title: Part 2: Sentence Scramble
sections.instructions: Tap the words in the correct order to build the sentence.

items.q: I am reading Japanese a little every day.
items.explanation: 毎日 + 日本語 + を (object) + 少し + 読んでいます. 読んでいます = te-form of 読む (読んで, godan む-verb: む → んで) + います = progressive 'is reading' (G8). を marks 日本語 as the direct object of 読む. 少し (N5.14) = a little. 多く would be the adverbial form of 多い ('a lot/plentifully') — the antonym of 少し. 書いて is the te-form of 書く (write) — wrong verb here. に marks a destination or time, not a direct object. 毎日 can float: 日本語を毎日少し読んでいます is equally natural.

items.q: Please write your name here by hand.
items.explanation: ここ + に (location) + 名前 + を (object) + 手 + で (means: by hand) + 書いてください. 書いてください = te-form of 書く + ください = polite request 'please write' (G7). に marks ここ as the location where the writing takes place. で marks 手 (N5.13) as the instrument/means — 手で = 'by hand.' を marks 名前 as the direct object of 書く. が would mark 名前 as a subject — wrong role. 話して is the te-form of 話す (speak) — wrong verb. から marks a starting point — does not fit.

items.q: I don't have much time today, but I will speak on the phone.
items.explanation: 今日 + は (topic) + 時間 + が (subject) + 少ないです + が (p_ga_but: clause connector 'but') + 電話 + で (means: by phone) + 話します. Note: the sentence contains two が — the first is p_ga (subject marker before 少ないです), the second is p_ga_but (clause connector 'but' after 少ないです, G9). 少ない (N5.14) = scarce/not much. 電話で = 'by phone/on the phone.' に would mark a destination — not the instrument. 多い (N5.14) = many/a lot — antonym of 少ない. 書きます = 'will write' — wrong verb here.

items.q: Are you reading that white book?
items.explanation: あの + 白い (N5.14, attributive i-adjective modifying 本) + 本 + を (object) + 読んでいます + か (question). 読んでいます = progressive 'is reading' (G8). 白い in attributive position: the surface form of i-adjectives is unchanged when modifying a noun directly (白い本 = white book). を marks 本 as the direct object of 読む. が would mark 本 as subject — wrong role here. 書いて is the te-form of 書く (write) — wrong verb. 多い = 'many' — an i-adjective but describes quantity, not colour.

items.q: Please speak slowly every day.
items.explanation: 毎日 + ゆっくり + 話してください. 話してください = te-form of 話す (話して) + ください = polite request 'please speak' (G7). ゆっくり (N5.13) = slowly/leisurely — an adverb modifying the verb. 毎日 can appear before or after ゆっくり: ゆっくり毎日話してください is also natural. 書いてください = 'please write' (書く te-form + ください) — correct structure but wrong verb. 少し = 'a little' — not the same as 'slowly.' に is a particle for destination/time — does not fit.

items.q: I am usually speaking Japanese at school.
items.explanation: たいてい + 学校 + で (location of action) + 日本語 + を (object) + 話しています. 話しています = te-form of 話す (話して) + います = progressive/habitual 'is speaking' (G8). で marks 学校 as where the action takes place. たいてい (N5.14) = usually/mostly — a frequency adverb that can float: both たいてい学校で and 学校でたいてい are natural. に marks a destination (学校に行く) — not the location of an ongoing activity. 読んでいます = 'is reading' — wrong verb. 書いて = te-form of 書く — wrong verb.

items.q: There are probably many people at the station today.
items.explanation: 多分 + 今日 + は (topic) + 駅 + に (location of existence) + 人 + が (subject) + 多いです. 多分 (N5.14) = probably — a degree adverb for uncertainty that naturally appears sentence-initially or after the topic expression. に marks 駅 as where the people exist (X に Y が います/あります). 多い (N5.14) = many/numerous. 少ない (N5.14) = few/scarce — antonym of 多い. で marks a location of action — not existence. から marks a starting point — does not fit. 多分 can follow the topic: 今日は多分駅に人が多いです is also natural.

sections.title: Part 3: Reading — Reading and Writing
sections.instructions: Read the passage and answer the questions.

passage.jp: わたしは 毎日 少し 本を 読みます。
passage.en: I read books a little every day.

passage.jp: 電話で 話すことも すきですが、手で 書くことが いちばん すきです。
passage.en: I also like talking on the phone, but I like writing by hand the most.

passage.jp: 今日は 時間が 少ないですが、少し 書きました。
passage.en: Today I don't have much time, but I wrote a little.

passage.jp: たいてい 時間が 多いですから、ゆっくり 読みます。
passage.en: I usually have a lot of time, so I read slowly.

passage.jp: 多分 今日も ゆっくり 話します。
passage.en: I will probably also speak slowly today.

questions.q: 何が いちばん すきですか。
questions.a: 手で 書くことが いちばん すきです。
choices: 手で 書くことが いちばん すきです。
電話で 話すことが いちばん すきです。
毎日 読むことが いちばん すきです。
ゆっくり 話すことが いちばん すきです。
questions.explanation: The passage states 手で書くことが一番すきです — 'I like writing by hand the most.' 一番 (N5.1) = most/best. 手で = 'by hand' (v_te + p_de, instrument particle). 書くこと = the act of writing (v_kaku plain form + こと nominalizer, early-use from N5.9, written as hiragana こと). が marks 書くこと as the subject of すきです. The other choices also use こと nominalization: 電話で話すこと (talking on the phone — mentioned but not 一番), 毎日読むこと (reading every day — mentioned in Line 1), ゆっくり話すこと (speaking slowly — Line 5).

questions.q: 今日 時間が 多いですか。
choices: はい、たくさんあります。
いいえ、ぜんぜんありません。
questions.explanation: Line 3 states 今日は時間が少ないです — 'Today I don't have much time.' 少ない (N5.14) = scarce/few. The が in 今日は時間が少ない is p_ga (subject marker) — 時間 is the subject of 少ない. The correct answer is いいえ、少ないです (No, there is little time). はい、多いです contradicts Line 3 — 多い (many) is stated as たいてい (usually) in Line 4, not for today. ぜんぜんありません = 'there is none at all' — too strong; the passage says 少し書きました (wrote a little), so some time existed. ぜんぜん (N5.14) = not at all — used with negative verbs.

questions.q: 今日も ゆっくり 話しますか。
questions.a: はい、多分 ゆっくり 話します。
choices: はい、多分 ゆっくり 話します。
いいえ、今日は 話しません。
はい、毎日 電話で 話します。
いいえ、ゆっくり 書きます。
questions.explanation: Line 5 states 多分今日もゆっくり話します — 'I will probably also speak slowly today.' 多分 (N5.14) = probably — expresses uncertainty about a future action. も (p_mo) marks 今日 as 'also' or 'too' — in addition to other days. So the narrator expects to speak slowly today as well, consistent with their usual habit. いいえ、今日は話しません contradicts Line 5. はい、毎日電話で話します is too strong — 電話 is mentioned in Lines 2 and 5, but 毎日 (every day) for 電話 is not stated. いいえ、ゆっくり書きます contradicts the verb — Line 5 says 話す (speak), not 書く (write).

sections.title: Part 4: Kanji Readings
sections.instructions: Select the correct reading for the [bracketed] word.

items.q: 毎日 [手] で 書いてください。
items.explanation: 手 (hand) is read as te (kun-reading). 手 (しゅ/て) — kun-reading て. 手で = 'by hand' — instrument particle で after the body-part noun 手. On-reading しゅ is used in compounds like 選手 (せんしゅ = athlete). Compare: か is the kun-reading of 書 (write), はな is the kun-reading of 話 (speak/story) — both other N5.13 kanji. Introduced in N5.13 as part of the communication lesson.

items.q: 日本語を [書いて] ください。
items.explanation: 書いて (write, te-form) — 書 is read as ka (kun-reading). 書く (write, godan く-verb) te-form: く → いて, so 書 + いて = 書いて. On-reading しょ in compounds like 読書 (どくしょ = reading books). Compare: よんで = 読んで (te-form of 読む = read), はなして = 話して (te-form of 話す = speak), いって = 言って (te-form of 言う = say) — all N5.13 communication verbs in te-form. Introduced in N5.13.

items.q: 先生に [言いました]。
items.explanation: 言いました (said) — 言 is read as i (kun-reading). 言う = to say (godan う-verb; 言 + う → 言う, pronounced いう). Polite past: 言い (stem) + ました = 言いました. On-reading げん in 言語 (げんご = language). Compare: 書きました (wrote), 読みました (read), 話しました (spoke) — all polite past forms of N5.13 communication verbs. Introduced in N5.13.

items.q: この 本を [読む] のが すきです。
items.explanation: 読む (read) is read as yomu (kun-reading). 読 (どく/よ) — kun-reading よ (read). 読む = to read (godan む-verb). On-reading どく in 読書 (どくしょ = reading books). 読むの = nominalized verb phrase 'the act of reading' — 読む in plain form + の = nominalizer. Compare: 書く (かく = write), 話す (はなす = speak), 言う (いう = say) — all communication verbs from N5.13.

items.q: 先生と [話します]。
items.explanation: 話します (will speak) — 話 is read as hanashi (kun-reading). 話 (わ/はな) — kun-reading はな (speak/story). 話す = to speak/talk (godan す-verb). Polite present/future: 話し + ます = 話します. On-reading わ in 電話 (でんわ = telephone): 電 (N5.10) + 話 (N5.13). Compare: 書きます (write), 言います (say), 読みます (read) — all polite non-past forms of N5.13 communication verbs.

items.q: 時間が [少し] あります。
items.explanation: 少し (a little) is read as sukoshi. 少 (しょう/すこ/すく) — kun-reading すこ in 少し. 少し = 'a little / slightly.' Compare: 少ない (すくない = scarce, few) uses the other kun-reading すく of 少. おおく = 多く (adverbial of 多い = many, a lot) — opposite in quantity; uses 多 (N5.14). しろく = 白く (adverbial of 白い = white) — different N5.14 kanji. たぶん = 多分 (probably) — written using 多 (N5.14) + 分 (N5.4), but is a completely different word. Introduced in N5.14.

items.q: 駅に 人が [多い] です。
items.explanation: 多い (many) is read as ooi. 多 (た/おお) — kun-reading おお in 多い. 多い = 'many/numerous' (i-adjective). On-reading た in 多分 (たぶん = probably). Compare: 少ない (すくない = scarce, few) is the direct antonym of 多い — both N5.14 i-adjectives of quantity. 白い (しろい = white) uses the N5.14 kanji 白, but describes colour not quantity. たいてい = 'usually' — a separate kana-only word, not a reading of 多. Introduced in N5.14.

items.q: [白い] 本を 読みます。
items.explanation: 白い (white) is read as shiroi. 白 (はく/しろ) — kun-reading しろ. 白い = 'white' (i-adjective). On-reading はく in 白人 (はくじん = Caucasian person): 白 (N5.14) + 人 (N5.1). Note: はくい is not a real word — this tests that students know the correct kun-reading しろ (not on-reading はく) for the adjective 白い. Compare: 多い (おおい = many), 少ない (すくない = scarce) — other N5.14 i-adjectives. Introduced in N5.14.

items.q: 駅と 学校の [間] に あります。
items.explanation: 間 (between/space) is read as aida (kun-reading). 間 (かん/けん/あいだ/ま) — kun-reading あいだ when used spatially for 'the space between.' Pattern: 〜と〜の間に = 'between X and Y.' On-reading かん in 時間 (じかん = time, N5.14). Reading ま in time expressions like 間に合う. Compare: 前 (まえ = in front, N5.9), 外 (そと = outside, N5.9), 中 (なか = inside, N5.9) — all relative-position words from N5.9. Introduced in N5.14.

sections.title: Part 5: Context & Vocabulary
sections.instructions: Select the best word to complete the sentence.

items.q: _______ 話してください。よく 分かります。
items.explanation: ゆっくり (N5.13) = slowly/leisurely — the adverb that naturally pairs with a request to speak: ゆっくり話してください = 'Please speak slowly.' よく分かります = 'You understand well' (positive result). たいてい = 'usually' — a frequency adverb (N5.14), not a manner adverb; たいてい話してください would mean 'please usually speak,' which is unnatural here. ぜんぜん = 'not at all' — used with negatives: ぜんぜん分かりません (I don't understand at all); not with requests. たくさん = 'a lot' — modifies quantities, not the manner of speaking.

items.q: 日本語が _______ 分かりません。（I don't understand Japanese at all.）
items.explanation: ぜんぜん (N5.14) = not at all — always pairs with a negative verb/adjective: ぜんぜん分かりません = 'I don't understand at all.' ぜんぜん with a negative is a strong intensifier. たいてい = 'usually' — a frequency adverb, not a total-negative intensifier; たいてい分かりません = 'I usually don't understand' (partial, not total). 少し = 'a little' — the opposite emphasis: 少し分かります = 'I understand a little' (positive). 多分 = 'probably' — expresses probability: 多分分かりません = 'I probably don't understand' (uncertain, not absolute).

items.q: わたしは _______ 学校で 日本語を 読んでいます。（I usually read Japanese at school.）
items.explanation: たいてい (N5.14) = usually/mostly — a frequency adverb for habitual actions: たいてい学校で日本語を読んでいます = 'I usually read Japanese at school.' ぜんぜん = 'not at all' — used with negatives; ぜんぜん読んでいません would mean 'not reading at all' — contradicts the positive sentence. 少し = 'a little' — a quantifier (少し読む = 'read a little'), not a frequency; it changes the meaning significantly. 多分 = 'probably' — expresses uncertainty about a fact, not a habitual frequency.

items.q: やまかわさんに _______ しましょう。（Let's call Yamakawa.）
items.explanation: 電話 (N5.13) = telephone — 電話する = to make a phone call (compound suru verb: v_denwa + v_suru). 電話しましょう = 'Let's call' (polite volitional, G8). に marks やまかわさん as the recipient. 空手 (N5.13) = karate — a martial art; 空手する is not a natural phrase for this context. 手 (N5.13) = hand — 手する is not a standard compound suru verb. 話 (N5.13) = speech/talk — 話する is not standard; the correct verb for talking is 話す or 電話で話す.

sections.title: Part 6: Grammar Check
sections.instructions: Choose the correct particle or form to complete the sentence.

items.q: ゆっくり 話して ___。（Please speak slowly.）
items.explanation: ください after the te-form creates a polite request (G7, N5.5+): 話して + ください = 'please speak.' This is the てください pattern: [verb te-form] + ください = polite request. います would create 話しています = 'is speaking' (progressive, G8) — a statement, not a request. いました = 話していました = 'was speaking' (past progressive) — past tense statement. みます = 話してみます = 'will try speaking' — uses the G23 てみる pattern (N4.35+) which is not yet available at this level.

items.q: 毎日 学校で 日本語を ___。（I am reading Japanese at school every day.）
items.explanation: 読んでいます = te-form of 読む (読んで, godan む-verb nasal euphonic change) + います = progressive/habitual 'is reading' (G8). With 毎日, ている describes an ongoing daily habit. 読みます = 'will read / reads' — simple polite non-past, describes a plan or general habit but not an ongoing action. 読みました = 'read' — past tense (G6). 読みましょう = 'let's read' — polite volitional/invitation (G8), not a statement about one's own habits.

items.q: 時間が 少ないです ___、電話で 話します。（I don't have much time, but I will talk on the phone.）
items.explanation: が (p_ga_but, G9) is the polite clause-connector 'but' — it follows a polite form (少ないです) to contrast two clauses within one sentence. In polite speech, が is the standard contrastive connector. でも is a sentence-STARTING conjunction — it begins a new sentence ('But I will...') and cannot link clauses within one sentence. から after 少ないです means 'because' (p_kara_because) — completely changes the meaning to 'because I don't have much time.' けど (p_kedo, G9) is also a 'but' connector but is casual in register — less appropriate after a polite form like ～ですが. In polite speech: use が; in casual speech: use けど.

items.q: 家 ___ 学校まで 電車で 行きます。（I go from home to school by train.）
items.explanation: から (p_kara, G3) after the noun 家 marks the starting point 'from' — 家から = 'from home.' Note: this から follows a NOUN, so it is p_kara ('from') — NOT p_kara_because (G9, which follows a verb/adjective). The pattern 〜から〜まで = 'from X to Y.' まで marks 学校 as the endpoint. で marks 電車 as the means of transport — 電車で = 'by train.' に marks a destination or existence location — 家に行く (go home) is correct but 家に学校まで = unnatural starting point. が marks the subject — not a location role.

items.q: きのうの テストは 長 ___。（Yesterday's test was long.）
items.explanation: かったです is the polite past suffix for i-adjectives (G10, N5.10+): [i-adj stem] + かったです. 長い → remove い → stem 長 + かったです = 長かったです (polite past: 'was long'). かいです — not a valid form; です is not added to a stem ending in か. いです = 長いです = 'is long' (present polite) — not past. くてです — くて is the te-form connector of i-adjectives (長くて = 'long and...') used to connect adjectives within a sentence — not a sentence-ending polite past form.
